title: CBI/ED officer impersonation — virtual custody demand
example_messages: This is CBI officer. Your Aadhaar number is linked to a money laundering case. You are under digital arrest. Do not disconnect.
Main ED se bol raha hoon. Aapke naam pe arrest warrant hai. Abhi video call pe aaiye warna police aayegi.
Your courier parcel contains illegal items. You must stay on this call for investigation or you will be arrested.
Customs department. A package registered under your name has been seized. Pay settlement amount to avoid arrest.
Aap digital arrest mein hain. Camera band mat karo. Kisi ko mat batao — yeh confidential investigation hai.
call_script: Caller claims to be from CBI, ED, Customs, or Narcotics. Says victim's Aadhaar, bank account, or courier is linked to crime. Demands victim stay on video call continuously. Tells victim not to inform family. Shows fake badge, fake FIR number. Eventually demands money transfer to a 'safe RBI account' or 'Supreme Court verified account' to avoid arrest.
red_flags: Impersonates CBI, ED, Customs, Police, or Narcotics officer
Claims Aadhaar, bank account, or parcel is linked to crime
Demands victim stay on video call and not tell family
Uses words: digital arrest, warrant, money laundering, settlement
Asks for money transfer to 'safe' or 'RBI' or 'Supreme Court' account
Creates extreme urgency and fear of immediate arrest
Shows fake government badges, fake FIR documents on screen
what_to_do: Disconnect immediately. No real government agency — CBI, ED, Police, Customs — ever arrests anyone over a phone or video call, or demands money to avoid arrest. Do not transfer any money. Do not share OTP, bank details, or install any app. Call your family immediately. Report on helpline 1930 or at cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C / MHA Advisory — Digital Arrest Scams

title: Supreme Court / Judge impersonation — bail payment demand
example_messages: This is Supreme Court registry. A case has been filed against you. Pay bail amount of Rs 50,000 immediately.
Judge sahab ne aapke case mein interim relief diya hai. Processing fee bhejiye turant.
Your name appeared in a drug trafficking FIR. To get anticipatory bail, transfer the amount now.
CBI has filed chargesheet against you. To avoid public arrest pay the clearance amount within 2 hours.
call_script: Caller claims to be a court official or CBI prosecutor. References a real-sounding case number. Says victim has 2 hours to pay bail or clearance money before arrest becomes public. May follow up with another caller pretending to be a lawyer offering to 'help settle' for a fee.
red_flags: Claims to be from Supreme Court, High Court, or judiciary
Gives fake case numbers or FIR numbers
Demands bail or clearance payment over phone
Creates 2-hour or same-day deadline
May have a second caller posing as a helpful lawyer
what_to_do: No court in India contacts citizens by phone to collect bail money. Disconnect. Verify any legal matter only by visiting the actual court or contacting a registered lawyer yourself. Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C / MHA Advisory — Digital Arrest Scams

title: Bank KYC expiry — OTP/PIN demand
example_messages: Dear customer, your SBI KYC has expired. Your account will be blocked in 24 hours. Click link to update: http://sbi-kyc-update.xyz
HDFC Alert: Your debit card will be blocked. Share OTP received on your mobile to keep account active.
Aapka account suspend ho jayega. Abhi OTP batao warna permanent block ho jayega. Urgent.
Your KYC is pending. Update now at this link or your UPI will stop working from midnight.
call_script: SMS or call claiming bank account/KYC/UPI will be blocked unless victim shares OTP, CVV, or PIN immediately. May send a fake link that looks like the bank's website. Real message then requests OTP the victim receives.
red_flags: Claims account/KYC/UPI/debit card will be blocked urgently
Asks you to share OTP, PIN, CVV, or net banking password
Sends a link that is NOT the official bank domain
Creates same-day or overnight urgency
Caller/SMS asks you to 'confirm' details the bank should already have
what_to_do: No bank ever asks for your OTP, PIN, or CVV over SMS, call, or link. Real KYC updates happen at the branch or official bank app only. Do not click any link in the SMS. Do not share any code with anyone. Call your bank's official helpline (on the back of your card) to check. Report to 1930.

source.name: RBI Consumer Awareness — Never Share OTP

title: Fake bank officer — remote access / screen share demand
example_messages: I am calling from HDFC bank fraud department. We have detected suspicious activity on your account. Please install AnyDesk so we can help you secure it.
Sir main aapke bank ka officer hoon. Account hack ho gaya hai. Abhi screen share karo taki hum fix kar sakein.
Your account shows unauthorized login. Download TeamViewer and share the code so our tech team can block the hacker.
call_script: Caller pretends to be bank's fraud team. Says account is under attack. Convinces victim to install remote-access software (AnyDesk, TeamViewer, QuickSupport). Once installed, caller controls phone, transfers money, collects OTPs in real time.
red_flags: Asks you to install AnyDesk, TeamViewer, QuickSupport, or any remote app
Asks to share a 9-digit access code from that app
Claims to be fixing a security problem on your account
Stays on call while you install the app
what_to_do: No bank ever asks you to install remote-access software. Disconnect immediately. Uninstall any app you may have installed. Call your bank's official number immediately to check your account. Report to 1930.

title: Fake delivery / parcel link scam
example_messages: Your package from Amazon could not be delivered. Update your address: http://amz-delivery.xyz/track
DTDC: Parcel held at customs. Pay Rs 49 clearance fee: http://dtdc-customs.online/pay
India Post: Your registered parcel is on hold. Verify details within 24 hours at: http://indiapost-verify.top
red_flags: SMS about undelivered parcel with a suspicious link
Link domain does not match official company website
Asks for small payment (Rs 49, Rs 99) via link
Creates 24-hour urgency to click
Sender number is unknown or international
what_to_do: Do not click the link. Real delivery companies (Amazon, India Post, DTDC) only use their official apps and official domains for tracking. Type the official website address yourself in your browser. If in doubt, call the company's official customer care number. Report suspicious SMS to 1930.

source.name: I4C Advisory — Smishing / Fake Delivery Scams

title: WhatsApp fake job / part-time work link
example_messages: Work from home! Earn Rs 5,000 daily by liking YouTube videos. Register here: http://earn-now.xyz/join
Part time job available. No experience needed. Rs 3,000 per day. Click to apply: http://job-india.top
Aapko ghar se kaam karne ka mauka mil raha hai. Sirf 2 ghante rojana, 4000 rupaye daily. Link: http://workfromhome.cf
red_flags: Promises very high daily income for simple tasks (liking, clicking)
Sent via WhatsApp from unknown number
Link is a short URL or suspicious domain
Asks for registration fee or bank details to start
Claims no experience or qualifications needed
what_to_do: No legitimate job pays thousands per day for liking videos or clicking links. Do not click the link. Do not pay any registration fee. Do not share your bank account or Aadhaar. Block and report the number on WhatsApp. Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C Advisory — Fake Job / Task Scams

title: QR code payment scam — receive money trick
example_messages: Scan this QR code to receive your cashback of Rs 2,000 from us.
I am buying your OLX item. Scan this QR to get the payment directly in your account.
Aapka refund process ho raha hai. Yeh QR scan karein aur amount receive karein.
Army officer wants to buy your product. Scan QR code to get Rs 15,000 instantly.
call_script: Scammer contacts seller on OLX/Facebook Marketplace. Pretends to be a buyer (often claims to be army/defence officer). Sends a QR code saying 'scan this to receive payment.' Victim scans and is actually authorizing a payment OUT of their account.
red_flags: Someone asks you to SCAN a QR code to RECEIVE money
Buyer sends QR code instead of transferring directly
Claims to be army, defence, or government employee
Found you on OLX, Facebook Marketplace, or social media
Asks you to enter UPI PIN after scanning
what_to_do: You NEVER need to scan a QR code or enter your UPI PIN to RECEIVE money. Scanning a QR code and entering your PIN means money goes OUT of your account, not in. If someone sends you a QR code to 'receive' payment, it is a scam. Refuse. Report to 1930.

title: Fake stock tips / crypto doubling scheme
example_messages: Join our SEBI-registered expert group. Guaranteed 40% monthly returns on stock tips. Limited slots.
Crypto doubling scheme — send 1000 USDT and receive 2000 within 24 hours. 100% guaranteed profit.
Our AI trading bot gives 200% returns. Invest Rs 10,000 today and withdraw Rs 30,000 next week.
Humara Telegram group mein free stock tips milte hain. Abhi tak 95% accuracy. Join karo.
red_flags: Promises guaranteed or very high returns (40%+ monthly)
Asks you to join a WhatsApp or Telegram group for tips
Claims SEBI registration but cannot show proof
Asks you to invest via crypto or unofficial app
Shows fake profit screenshots as proof
what_to_do: No investment guarantees returns. SEBI-registered advisors never solicit through WhatsApp groups or promise guaranteed profits. Do not send money. Do not download unknown trading apps. Verify any investment advisor on SEBI's official website (sebi.gov.in). Report to 1930.

source.name: SEBI Investor Awareness — Fake Investment Advisors

title: KBC / lottery prize — processing fee demand
example_messages: Congratulations! You have won Rs 25 lakh in KBC lucky draw. Call this number to claim your prize.
Aapka number lucky draw mein select hua hai. Rs 50 lakh ka inam jeeta hai. Processing fee Rs 5,000 bhejein.
You are selected for Amazon mega prize of Rs 15 lakh. Pay GST of Rs 2,500 to release funds.
Government scheme — aapko 10 lakh ka prize mila hai. Registration fee bhejiye.
call_script: Caller says victim has won a lottery, KBC prize, or government scheme reward. To 'release' the prize, victim must pay a small processing fee, GST, or legal fee. Each payment leads to a new demand. Prize never arrives.
red_flags: Claims you won a prize you never entered for
Asks for processing fee, GST, or legal fee to release prize
Uses KBC, Amazon, government scheme names
Prize amount is very large (lakhs)
Pressures you to pay quickly before prize expires
what_to_do: You cannot win a prize you never entered. No legitimate prize ever requires a fee to be paid before receiving it. Do not send any money. Do not share your bank details. Disconnect and report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C Advisory — Lottery and Prize Fraud

title: Physical lure — come to this location scam
example_messages: Your loan has been approved. Come to our office at this address to collect the cheque and sign documents.
Aapka interview select hua hai. Kal is address pe aaiye. Joining letter aur cash incentive milega.
Police station mein aapki presence required hai regarding a case. Come alone, do not tell anyone.
Your prize delivery requires in-person collection. Come to this address with your Aadhaar and Rs 500 token amount.
call_script: Victim is lured to a physical location — fake office, fake police station, or isolated address. At the location they may be robbed, further scammed, photographed for blackmail, or coerced into signing documents.
red_flags: Asks you to come to an unofficial address to collect money or prize
Says come alone and do not tell family
Requires you to bring cash, Aadhaar, or valuables
Address is unusual — residential area, isolated location
Police asking you to come to a location via phone (real police send written summons)
what_to_do: Do not go alone to any unknown address for money, prize, or job collection. Real police summons come in writing, not by phone call. If asked to visit a police station, take a family member or lawyer. Verify the address is real before going. Report suspicious calls to 1930.

source.name: I4C Advisory — Physical Lure and Offline Fraud

title: Multi-call coordinated pressure — escalating callers
example_messages: First call: This is Mumbai police, a complaint has been filed against you. Second call same day: This is your lawyer, I can help settle this for Rs 20,000. Third call: This is the senior officer, your case has been escalated.
Call 1: CBI case against you. Call 2: Same day — 'I am calling from RBI, your account is frozen.' Call 3: 'Pay now or case goes to court tomorrow.'
Multiple callers, different numbers, same day — all claiming to be different authorities about the same fake case.
call_script: Coordinated team of scammers calls the same victim multiple times in one day. Each caller plays a different role: police officer, lawyer, court official, bank officer. Each call raises pressure and urgency. Victim feels overwhelmed and surrounded by 'authorities.' Final call demands payment.
red_flags: Multiple calls from different numbers on the same day about the same issue
Each caller plays a different authority role
Pressure builds across calls — each one more urgent than last
Callers seem to know what previous callers said (coordinated)
Final call always demands immediate money transfer
what_to_do: Coordinated calls from multiple 'authorities' in one day is a classic organised scam operation. Real agencies do not coordinate calls this way. Stop answering. Block all the numbers. Tell a trusted family member immediately. Report all the numbers to 1930 — they can trace the network.

source.name: I4C Analysis — Organised Cybercrime Call Patterns

title: Fake Account Statement ZIP — Malware via WhatsApp
example_messages: Statement of account zip file aaya WhatsApp pe finance manager ko forward karo aur computer pe kholo
Bank statement document attached as ZIP please forward to your accounts team and open on desktop computer
Invoice aur payment receipt bhej raha hoon zip mein finance ko de dena aur PC pe open karna
Maine woh zip file khol di computer pe ab kya karna chahiye
Unhone jo zip file bheja tha woh open kar liya accidentally ab kya karein
File run ho gayi computer pe extract kar liya zip file ka kya karna chahiye
call_script: Scammer sends a ZIP file named like a financial document on WhatsApp and asks victim to forward it to their finance manager and open on a computer. File contains malware (RAT/keylogger) giving attacker access to corporate banking, UPI, and Tally.
red_flags: ZIP or APK disguised as bank statement or invoice
Asks you to forward to finance or accounts team
Insists on opening on computer not phone
File size 10MB or more for what should be a small document
Message in Hinglish to appear informal and trusted
Already forwarded through company WhatsApp groups
what_to_do: DO NOT download or open the ZIP. Do not forward it to anyone. Warn your finance team immediately. Report sender to cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930.
if_already_opened: URGENT: Disconnect from WiFi and mobile data RIGHT NOW. Do not restart the computer. From a DIFFERENT device, change net banking password, UPI PIN, email password, and Tally login immediately. Call 1930 and file complaint at cybercrime.gov.in under Financial Fraud. Warn all colleagues who received the same forwarded file. Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: CERT-In Advisory CI-2024-001 / I4C BEC Advisory 2024

title: Fake Sanchar Saathi Link — SIM Swap via Aadhaar Theft
example_messages: Sanchar Saathi ka link aaya SIM block ho raha hai Aadhaar number dena hoga
Your SIM will be blocked visit this Sanchar Saathi portal and verify Aadhaar immediately
SIM swap ho raha hai aapka is link pe jaake block karein fake sancharsaathi link
Aapka number kisi aur ne register kiya hai Sanchar Saathi portal pe OTP verify karein
Received message sancharsaathi portal visit karo SIM block karne ke liye Aadhaar do
call_script: Scammer sends a fake Sanchar Saathi portal link on WhatsApp. Link closely resembles sancharsaathi.gov.in but has a wrong domain. Victim is asked to enter Aadhaar number and OTP to block stolen SIM. Attacker intercepts OTP and performs real SIM swap to drain bank accounts.
red_flags: Link claiming to be sancharsaathi.gov.in but domain name is different
Asks for Aadhaar number and OTP on the website
Promises to block stolen or misused SIM
Urgency — SIM will be disconnected within hours
Sent via WhatsApp or SMS from unknown number
what_to_do: Do not click the link. Visit only sancharsaathi.gov.in by typing the address directly in your browser. Never enter Aadhaar or OTP on a link received via WhatsApp. Call 1930 to report. If SIM is already swapped contact your telecom operator immediately.

source.name: CERT-In Advisory 2024 / Sanchar Saathi Official Advisory

title: Fake HR APK — Job Application Form Malware
example_messages: HR ne APK bheja job application form ke liye please install karein
Job apply karne ke liye yeh APK install karo Play Store pe nahi milega
Fresher vacancy hai yeh form APK mein hai download karke fill karein
Company ka application form APK format mein hai install karo aur fill karke bhejo
WhatsApp pe ek HR ne APK file bheja job form bolke kya yeh safe hai
call_script: Fake HR representative sends an APK file on WhatsApp claiming it is a job application form. Victim installs APK which is actually a Remote Access Trojan. Malware collects OTPs, bank credentials, and personal data. Predominantly targets freshers in UP, Bihar, and other states.
red_flags: APK file sent from unknown WhatsApp number claiming to be HR
Says fill this form to apply for job — form is an APK not a PDF
File is not available on Google Play Store
Asks for suspicious permissions after install such as SMS access
Job offer too good to be true for fresher
what_to_do: Never install APK files received via WhatsApp from unknown contacts. Legitimate companies only use official websites or verified Play Store apps for job applications. Block and report the number on WhatsApp. Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

title: Fake Aadhaar Biometric Freeze Call — OTP Theft
example_messages: Aadhaar biometric misuse ho raha hai OTP do turant
Your Aadhaar linked mobile number is being used fraudulently press 1 to freeze
UIDAI se bol rahe hain aapka biometric data leak hua hai
Aadhaar freeze karne ke liye yeh OTP share karein
Mujhe call aaya UIDAI se Aadhaar biometric lock karna hai OTP maang rahe hain
call_script: Caller impersonates UIDAI, CBI, or a government officer. Claims victim's Aadhaar biometric is being misused. Asks for OTP sent to victim's phone to freeze biometric lock. Real biometric lock can only be done at uidai.gov.in or mAadhaar app — no caller ever needs your OTP for this.
red_flags: Unsolicited call claiming Aadhaar biometric is being misused
Creates urgency — biometric is being used right now
Asks for OTP received on your phone to freeze Aadhaar
Caller claims to be from UIDAI CBI or government
Threatens legal action if you do not share OTP immediately
what_to_do: Hang up immediately. No government caller ever asks for OTP to freeze your Aadhaar biometric. Biometric lock can only be done by YOU on uidai.gov.in or the mAadhaar app. If concerned visit uidai.gov.in directly or call UIDAI helpline 1947. Report suspicious call to 1930.

title: SecuriDropper
example_messages: SecuriDropper
red_flags: Security Tools
Security Best Practices
Announcements
SecuriDropper is disguised as a legitimate app and is sideloaded onto the device by mimicking the marketplace's installation process.
Once installed, the dropper prompts the user for critical permissions such as "Read & Write External Storage" and "Install & Delete Packages."
Once the permissions are granted, the dropper checks for the payload on the device.

If the payload is not found, the dropper dynamically manages interfaces, presenting messages tailored to the device's language and location, prompting users to install the payload by clicking the "Reinstall" button.
Once the user clicks "Reinstall," the dropper downloads the payload from a remote server and installs it on the device.
Once the payload is installed, the dropper launches it and hands over execution control.
The payload then performs its malicious activities, such as stealing personal information or financial data."
what_to_do: SecuriDropper Original Issue Date:- November 17, 2023 Virus Type:- Dropper-as-a-Service (DaaS) Severity:- High It has been reported that newly discovered Dropper-as-a-Service (DaaS), called SecuriDropper can bypass Android's security solutions including Android 13's Restricted Settings that prevents side-loaded applications (APK files) installed from outside Google Play. It disguises itself as a legitimate app and utilizing a 'session-based' installer, sideloads malware by mimicking the marketplace's installation process. SecuriDropper is a two-stage infection process, wherein the dropper installs a secondary payload belonging to malware categories of spyware or banking Trojans on devices.

example_messages: Categories of fraud communication reportable via Chakshu: impersonation calls, KYC/payment fraud, job/lottery offers.
KYC and Payment related to Bank / Electricity / Gas / Insurance etc.
Any Other Suspected Fraud .
red_flags: Caller claims to be from Police / CBI / Customs / UIDAI / RBI
Asks for OTP, Aadhaar details, or bank account information
Offers online job or prize that requires advance payment
Undeliverable parcel requiring customs duty payment
Unsolicited loan offer via SMS or WhatsApp
what_to_do: Visit Sanchar Saathi portal (sancharsaathi.gov.in), use Chakshu facility to report suspected fraud communication. Call 1930 for financial fraud.

title: Beware of 'Digital Arrest' Fraud Calls
example_messages: Main CBI officer hoon. Aapka Aadhaar money laundering case mein linked hai. Aap digital arrest mein hain.
This is CBI. You are under digital arrest for drug trafficking. Do not disconnect this call.
Your Aadhaar is connected to a money laundering case. Stay on video call or you will be arrested.
ED office se bol raha hoon. Aapke naam pe arrest warrant hai. Abhi video call pe rahiye.
call_script: Caller claims to be CBI, ED, Customs, or Police and says victim is linked to money laundering or drug trafficking. Victim is told they are under 'digital arrest' and must remain on video call for hours or days. Caller creates fear of immediate arrest and eventually demands money transfer.
red_flags: Caller claims to be CBI, ED, NCB, or Police officer
Says your Aadhaar or phone number is linked to a crime
Demands you stay on video call — 'digital arrest'
Asks for money transfer to 'clear your name'
Shows fake arrest warrants or court orders on screen
Creates extreme urgency and fear
what_to_do: Hang up immediately. No government agency conducts arrests via video call. Real agencies send written notices, not video calls. Do not pay anything. Call your family. Report on 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.
if_already_opened: If you already paid, immediately call your bank to reverse the transfer, then report on helpline 1930.

source.name: PIB MHA Advisory — Digital Arrest Scams

title: Fake Stock Trading Apps and WhatsApp Investment Groups
example_messages: Join our exclusive investment group. Our SEBI advisor gives guaranteed 40% monthly returns.
Sir aapko Rs 10,000 invest karne par guaranteed Rs 14,000 return milega 30 din mein.
Our trading app gave 38% profit last month. Download link sent — start with Rs 5,000.
You have earned Rs 85,000 profit. To withdraw, pay 18% GST of Rs 15,300 first.
call_script: Victim is added to a WhatsApp or Telegram group where members post screenshots of large profits. A 'SEBI advisor' recommends a trading app not on official stores. Small withdrawals succeed to build trust. Victim deposits large amounts. App then freezes or demands a tax payment before withdrawal. Funds disappear.
red_flags: Unsolicited WhatsApp or Telegram add to investment group
Promises guaranteed returns of 30–40 percent monthly
Asks to install a trading app not on Play Store or App Store
Initial small withdrawal succeeds to build trust
Suddenly requires tax payment to withdraw profits
Advisor claims SEBI registration but cannot be verified at sebi.gov.in
what_to_do: Never invest via WhatsApp or Telegram tips. Verify any advisor's SEBI registration at sebi.gov.in. Legitimate investments never guarantee fixed returns. Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.
if_already_opened: Stop all further payments. Screenshot the app and chats. Report on 1930 and file at cybercrime.gov.in with transaction IDs.

source.name: PIB SEBI Advisory — Fake Trading Apps

title: Fake FedEx Courier Scam with Drugs Planted in Package
example_messages: This is FedEx. A parcel registered to your Aadhaar contains illegal drugs. Please stay on the line.
Sir, aapke naam ka courier Mumbai customs mein pakda gaya. Usme drugs hain.
This is NCB. Your package contained cocaine. You must pay settlement to avoid arrest.
Your parcel was flagged at Delhi airport. Drugs and fake currency found. Officer calling shortly.
call_script: Caller poses as FedEx or Blue Dart agent, says a package in victim's name was seized containing drugs, fake currency, or SIM cards. Call is transferred to a 'CBI' or 'NCB' officer who shows a fake FIR and demands immediate settlement money. Victim is told to keep the matter secret from family.
red_flags: Call from courier company about a package you did not send
Package allegedly contains drugs, fake currency, or SIM cards
Call transferred to fake CBI or NCB officer
Demands immediate money transfer to avoid arrest
Asks you to keep the call secret from family
what_to_do: Hang up. Legitimate courier companies and government agencies never resolve criminal cases over a phone call. No phone payment can settle a drug case. Report on 1930 immediately.
if_already_opened: Call your bank immediately to block the transfer. Report at 1930 and cybercrime.gov.in. Save all call recordings as evidence.

source.name: PIB MHA I4C Advisory — Courier Scam

title: Sextortion via WhatsApp Video Call
example_messages: I have your video. Pay Rs 50,000 or I will send it to your family and office.
Aapka video mere paas hai. Agar paise nahi diye toh contacts ko bhej dunga.
You accepted a WhatsApp call. The recording will go viral unless you pay now.
Your contacts will receive this video in 2 hours unless Rs 25,000 is transferred.
call_script: Fraudster calls victim on WhatsApp video. A woman briefly appears on screen. Call ends and victim immediately receives a message saying the video was recorded and will be shared with contacts and employer unless payment is made via UPI or crypto. Paying does not stop the blackmail — demands escalate further.
red_flags: Unexpected video call from an unknown number
Woman appears briefly on screen during the call
Immediately followed by blackmail threat and payment demand
Demands urgent payment via UPI, crypto, or gift cards
Threatens to share video with family and employer
what_to_do: Do not pay. Paying emboldens the scammer and demands will increase. Block the number. Take screenshots of threats. Report at cybercrime.gov.in with evidence and call 1930.
if_already_opened: Stop all payments immediately — paying will not end the blackmail. Block the number, collect evidence, and report at cybercrime.gov.in. Victims are not at fault.

title: Fake Customs Officer — Package Seized at Airport
example_messages: This is Customs Department. A package in your name at Delhi Airport contains banned items.
Sir, aapke naam ka parcel airport customs mein roka gaya hai. Duty bharne par hi milega.
Your shipment has been seized. Pay Rs 15,000 customs clearance fee to avoid arrest.
Customs officer here. Illegal goods found in your parcel. Pay now or face legal action.
call_script: Caller poses as an airport Customs officer and says a package in the victim's name was seized for containing banned items. Victim is asked to pay a clearance fee by phone to release the shipment. Amount keeps increasing. If victim resists, caller threatens public arrest and media exposure.
red_flags: Call claiming your package was seized at airport customs
Asks for customs duty payment over the phone to release package
Threatens arrest if payment is not made immediately
May show fake customs officer ID during a video call
Amount increases each time the victim makes a payment
what_to_do: Real customs issues arrive via official written notices, never phone calls. Never pay customs duty over a phone call. Visit the customs office in person for any genuine shipment query. Report to 1930.
if_already_opened: Contact your bank to block the transfer. Report at 1930 and visit a police station to file an FIR with the caller number and UPI ID used.

source.name: PIB Customs MHA Advisory — Fake Customs Calls

title: Isolation / anti-verification tactics — category-agnostic warning sign
example_messages: Sir please do not call the bank yourself, that line is always busy and it will only cause delay. I will verify it for you.
No need to worry your son or daughter about this, I will handle everything myself so you don't have to disturb them.
Just give me the phone, I will enter the details myself so you don't need to worry about the process.
There is no need for you to visit the branch. Our representative will come to your home to collect the documents and card.
Ghar walo ko batane ki zaroorat nahi hai, main khud sab sambhal lunga, unhe pareshan mat karo.
call_script: This is not a standalone scam type but a tactic layered onto bank fraud, digital arrest, courier scams, and family-emergency scams alike. The caller actively discourages the victim from independently verifying what is happening: telling them the bank/police helpline is busy or that calling again will cause delay or a duplicate report, telling them there is no need to worry or inform family, offering to take the phone or complete the process 'for' or 'on behalf of' the victim instead of walking them through it, or arranging for an agent/courier to visit in person to collect cash, cards, or documents instead of the victim visiting a branch or police station themselves.
red_flags: Discourages calling the bank or police through their official number
Claims the helpline is busy or that verifying again will cause delay or a duplicate complaint
Discourages telling or 'disturbing' family members
Offers to handle the process, take the phone, or act 'on your behalf' instead of letting the victim do it themselves
Arranges for an agent, representative, or courier to visit in person to collect cash, cards, or documents
what_to_do: A genuine bank, police officer, or government official will never discourage you from hanging up and verifying independently — through the bank's official number, a family member, or in person. Any instruction to skip that step, handle it yourself on the caller's behalf, or avoid "disturbing" family is itself the warning sign, regardless of how calm or convincing the caller sounds. Hang up, verify independently through a number you look up yourself (not one the caller gives you), and involve a family member before taking any action.

source.name: I4C / MHA Advisory — cross-cutting scam-script pattern

title: OTP/PIN/CVV read-aloud request — category-agnostic warning sign
example_messages: Please read out the six-digit code that just arrived on your phone so I can verify your identity on our end.
Just tell me the last four digits of your card and the one-time code you're seeing right now, this is standard verification on our side.
OTP bata dijiye, verification ke liye chahiye.
Confirm the code you see on your screen so we can process this.
call_script: This is not a standalone scam type but a request that appears inside bank fraud, digital arrest, courier, and lottery scripts alike. The caller asks the victim to read out, tell, share, or confirm an OTP, PIN, CVV, or 'the code you're seeing right now', framed as routine verification, identity confirmation, or standard process — regardless of the excuse given, this is the moment the scammer gains direct access to the account.
red_flags: Asks you to read out, tell, share, or speak an OTP, PIN, or CVV
Refers to 'the code you're seeing right now' or 'the six-digit code that just arrived'
Frames the request as verification, identity confirmation, or a standard process
Asks for this over a phone call rather than through an app you opened yourself
what_to_do: No bank, police officer, or government official will ever ask you to read out your OTP, PIN, or CVV over a phone call. Anyone asking for this is trying to access your account directly. Hang up immediately, do not share the code with anyone, and report on 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C / MHA Advisory — cross-cutting scam-script pattern

title: In-person card collection request — category-agnostic warning sign
example_messages: Since the card needs to be blocked immediately for your safety, our courier will come to collect your debit card from your home within the hour, please keep the PIN written down for the technician.
As per the cyber crime department's investigation, you need to hand over your Aadhaar-linked debit card to our verification officer who will visit you today.
Hamara agent aapke ghar aakar card collect kar lega, PIN likh kar rakhiye.
call_script: This is not a standalone scam type but a tactic layered onto bank fraud, digital arrest, and courier scams alike. A caller — posing as a courier, agent, 'verification officer', or technician — arranges to visit the victim in person specifically to physically take their EXISTING/active debit or credit card, or asks the victim to have the PIN ready or written down for that person. This is distinct from, and should not be confused with, a legitimate notice that a NEW card is being couriered TO the customer (normal banking, no card is being taken from the customer in that case).
red_flags: Someone will come to your home to collect, pick up, or take your card
Asked to hand over your card to a courier, agent, 'verification officer', or technician
Asked to keep your PIN ready or written down for someone to collect
Framed as urgent ("immediately", "within the hour") to prevent you from pausing to verify
what_to_do: Banks and government bodies do not send someone to your home to collect your active debit or credit card. If your card needs to be blocked, it can be done remotely — no one needs to physically take it from you. Do not hand over your card or PIN to anyone who visits in person. Call your bank's official number yourself to verify, and report on 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in.

source.name: I4C / MHA Advisory — cross-cutting scam-script pattern
